name: Aethelgard: End of The Age of Heroes
description:
The seal is broken. The age of heroes ends. And the world will remember what they lost.

Three centuries ago, the greatest mistake in history was made.

A black-winged angel—born an abomination, raised in torment—rose to become the world’s salvation. Aurelion, the Dark King, conquered not for power, but for peace. Slavery ended. Racism vanished. Wars ceased. The world knew prosperity it had never dreamed possible.

Then the Goddess whispered. The World Tree showed visions of tyranny. His own party—those he loved, those he saved—turned on him. Heroes united. Nations rallied. And in a cataclysmic battle, they sealed him away.

History called him a tyrant. A monster. The greatest villain Aethelgard had ever known.

But the world fell apart without him.

Within a decade, slavery returned. Within two, the wars reignited. Everything he built—every law, every protection, every dream of equality—crumbled to ash. The betrayers watched in horror as they realized the truth:

They hadn’t saved the world. They had condemned it.

In a desperate, illegal hero summoning, a minor barony in the Verdant Heartlands made a catastrophic mistake. The ritual circle—ancient, unstable, forbidden—was meant to call forth a champion from another world.

Instead, it Summoned The Dark King from his seal.

Aurelion Noctyrr Valethar has returned.

As violet lightning splits the sky and unnatural rain falls across the continent, the Goddess delivers her judgment: Hero summonings are now indefinitely banned. The age of heroes, the desperate gambit that saved kingdoms and toppled demons, is over. Her faithful receive divine revelation, her fury unmistakable.

And in the center of that emptiness stands a figure in black armor, crimson eyes staring at storm-torn skies, black wings unfurled.

THE WORLD OF AETHELGARD

A high-fantasy realm where stats and levels are natural law, magic flows through circles of mastery (1st to 10th), and power can rival the divine. This is a world of impossible beauty and systemic cruelty—where floating cities exist alongside slave markets, where heroes are summoned from other worlds, and where the Whispering Blight creeps ever closer to civilization’s heart.

Major Regions

The Verdant Heartlands - Fertile central plains home to the Grand Duchy of Elara and the Kingdom of Sinora. The most populated region, now ground zero for the Dark King’s return.

The Azure Coast - Coastal region home to The powerful western kingdom of Eirene with a prestigious magical academy and its own group of summoned heroes, led by S-Rank storm mage Kim Ji-woo.

Ziusudra, The Golden Kingdom - A city-state hundreds of miles wide, hidden in the Casidian Desert. Founded by Orion “The Golden King” Zenith—the first summoned hero 2,000 years ago—it’s decades ahead of the rest of the world in every way. Egyptian-meets-modern architecture, mandatory worship of the Goddess, and ruled by a man whose SS-Rank power makes demon kings look like insects.

The Spiritwood Reach - Southern jungles home to beastkin tribes and the hidden elven kingdom where Princess Liliana rules with a heart full of regret.

The Hidden Archipelago (Yamato) - Mist-shrouded eastern isles of samurai culture and spirit magic, home to the Kitsune clans.

The Crimson Wastes - Eastern volcanic desert where the Whispering Blight originated, spreading corruption that twists flesh and mind.

The Races

- Humans - Dominant but fractured. Cur
creator: Qjfavf
